items.title: Johor religious authorities expected to issue fatwa on TikTok user’s teachings after investigation, says exco
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 10 — The Johor Mufti’s Department is expected to issue a fatwa regarding the teachings of a TikTok user who is being reviewed for alleged deviancy. Johor Islamic Religious Affairs Committee chairman Mohd Fared Mohd Khalid said that the Johor Islamic Religious Affairs Department (JAINJ) is currently conducting an investigation and research into the beliefs of the woman who uses the social media handle Ratu Nur Putri Ratu Balqis. Mohd Fared said that the woman’s social media posts, which disseminated her beliefs, have been taken down.

items.title: Umno among Asean's oldest political parties as it turns 80 - Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said Umno, which is approaching its 80th anniversary, remains one of the oldest political parties in the Asean region, with a legacy of struggle that reflects resilience and a key role in national development. The deputy prime minister said that throughout its long history, Umno has undergone various phases of struggle and shifts in the political landscape, reflecting its experience, resilience and key role in national development. Zahid, who is also Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman, posted this in conjunction with Umno's 80th anniversary.

items.title: Medicine supply stable, stocks sufficient until end-June, says Dzulkefly
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Health Ministry says Malaysia's medicine supply is stable and there is sufficient stock until the end of June despite the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said mitigation measures were already in place to ensure continued supply. He said the ministry has prepared mid-term and long-term strategies should the Middle East conflict persist.

items.title: Unemployment rate remains at 2.9pct in February - DOSM
items.summary: As such, he said, the labour force in that month increased 0.1 per cent to 17.30 million people compared to the previous month (January 2026: 17.28 million people), while the labour force participation rate for February remained at 70.9 per cent, unchanged from the previous month. On the number of employed persons for the month, Mohd Uzir said the upward trend recorded a slight increase of 0.1 per cent to 16.79 million people, up from 16.77 million people in January. "In addition, the number of self-employed people increased by 0.3 per cent to 3.13 million people compared to the previous month," he said.

items.title: Four charged with illegal possession of over 4,700kg cooking oil
items.summary: SEREMBAN: A local man and three Myanmar nationals, including one woman, were charged at the Sessions Court here today with illegally possessing more than 4,000kg of cooking oil on Tuesday. The accused are Chong Sze Thong, 52, and Myanmar nationals Thung Khaw, 27, Moe Myint Weh Yan, 17, and Nan Thet Khin, 32. Chong pleaded not guilty.

items.title: Pakistan defence minister says Israel a 'curse for humanity'
items.summary: HYDERABAD: Pakistan's Defence Minister Khawaja Asif strongly criticised Israel Zionist regime, accusing it of committing attacks on civilians across the region while peace talks are underway in Islamabad. "Israel is evil and a curse for humanity, while peace talks are underway in Islamabad, genocide is being committed in Lebanon," Asif said in a post on X. "Innocent citizens are being killed by Israel, first Gaza, then Iran and now Lebanon, bloodletting continues unabated," he added.

items.title: Malaysian Bar files bid to charge Zahid again in Yayasan Akalbudi case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Bar is seeking a court order to compel the Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC) to recharge Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi over all 47 charges of criminal breach of trust, corruption and money laundering involving Yayasan Akalbudi funds. According to court documents, the Bar claimed that the Attorney-General's decision to grant an NFA to the Umno president was tainted by irrationality, was unreasonable and contrary to public interest. "The decision to grant NFA was made without support from facts and evidence," it said.

items.title: Getha driving Malaysian rubber industry toward high-value global innovation
items.summary: "The most advanced latex formulation, known as Miracle Latex, offers up to four times greater elasticity compared to conventional materials and twice as much as other latex products in the market. "This latest achievement by Getha proves that Malaysia is not merely producing goods, but is also capable of creating world-class innovations that meet the highest global standards," she said during the launch of the Transforme Miracle Latex Prime Fibre mattress at Sunway Pyramid, Subang Jaya, recently. Also present was Getha project director, Kevin Tan.

items.title: Dzulkefly: Malaysia’s medicine supply stable until June despite rising costs
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 10 — The country’s medicine supply remains stable and sufficient at least until the end of June, thanks to combined government and industry stockpiles. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad said the government is preparing in case the West Asia conflict persists, including implementing medium- and long-term mitigation measures. He said supply chain disruptions, including delays in medical device shipments since before March, have also pushed up prices.

items.title: Anwar mourns death of ‘exceptional diplomat’ former Iranian foreign minister Kharazi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today expressed deep sorrow over the passing of former Iranian Foreign Minister Dr Kamal Kharazi, describing him as a man of rare intellect and genuine warmth, always thoughtful and gracious. He said Kharazi's untimely death resulted from wounds inflicted by Zionist terrorist attacks. “I first came to know Dr Kharazi during his years as Iran’s envoy to the United Nations in New York, and our friendship carried through his distinguished tenure as Foreign Minister.

items.title: Lego-style memes troll Trump after fragile US-Iran truce
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Shortly after news of a US-Iran ceasefire, an Iranian group released a new Lego-style video lampooning President Donald Trump and declaring "Iran won," the latest in a wave of war-themed AI-generated propaganda flooding the internet. Trump surrendered. "TACO will always remain TACO," it added, referring to the acronym "Trump always chickens out."

items.title: [UPDATED] Teen pleads not guilty to sexual offences against 10-year-old in Batu Caves
items.summary: The Form 5 student, who lives at the home, also pleaded not guilty to three charges of physical and non-physical sexual abuse involving the same boy. The sexual assault charges fall under Sections 14(b), 15(a)(i) and 15(a)(ii) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act. The physical sexual assault charge under Section 14(b), punishable under Section 14, carries a maximum 20-year jail term and whipping upon conviction.

items.title: Malaysians head to Thailand for Songkran water festival
items.summary: Just across the Sungai Golok in Sungai Golok town, opposite Rantau Panjang, preparations are in full swing for Thailand's most famous water festival. Songkran, which marks the Thai New Year, is known for water-splashing rituals symbolising cleansing, renewal and good fortune. Sungai Golok police chief Colonel Thun Sirikhunt said the festival will be held from April 11 to 14, with main celebrations on April 12 and 13.

items.title: ADB predicts slowing Asia growth as Middle East war takes toll
items.summary: MANILA: The Middle East war is expected to drag on Asia's economies over this year and next, the Asian Development Bank warned Friday, as it predicted growth to slow to 5.1 per cent across the world's most populous region. Growth predictions could fall to as low as 4.7 per cent for 2026 and 4.8 per cent in 2027 should the US-Israeli war with Iran drag into the third quarter. A more drawn-out conflict in the Middle East could also see inflation spike by as much as 5.6 per cent, the ADB said Friday.

items.title: IMF to cut global growth forecast due to Mideast war
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The International Monetary Fund will lower global growth forecasts due to the Middle East war, its chief said Thursday, warning of the conflict's "scarring effects" despite a fragile ceasefire. The IMF also anticipates having to provide up to US$50 billion in immediate financial assistance to countries affected by the war, with food insecurity set to affect at least 45 million people. "Given the spillovers from the war, we expect near-term demand for IMF balance-of-payments support to rise by somewhere between US$20 billion and US$50 billion, with the lower bound prevailing if ceasefire holds," Georgieva said.

items.title: India's TCS shares fall as rare annual revenue drop clouds outlook
items.summary: BENGALURU: Shares of Tata Consultancy Services fell nearly three per cent on Friday after a rare annual revenue drop outweighed strong deal wins and a quarterly earnings beat, suggesting sustained growth recovery remains elusive amid weak client spending and rising costs. The IT index was down 2.2 per cent, even as the Nifty 50 was trading 0.9 per cent higher. US-listed shares of TCS' smaller rivals Infosys and Wipro also lost nearly two per cent overnight.

items.title: Former 'mat rempit' turns a new leaf, earns five-figure income as content creator
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A former illegal street racer has turned his life around and now earns a five-figure monthly income as a motorcycle content creator, after once being labelled a "mat rempit" and ridiculed by society. Muhammad Khairul Khairuddin, 26, better known as Khairul ACQ among his social media followers, said he was previously involved in illegal street racing during his teenage years but has since left that lifestyle behind after becoming active as a motorcycle content creator on TikTok in 2017. Social media has transformed him from being unemployed and involved in illegal racing into a better person, he said.

items.title: Kenanga IB expects Bursa Malaysia's near-term trading momentum to remain supportive
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kenanga Investment Bank Bhd (Kenanga IB) believes trading momentum in Bursa Malaysia Bhd could remain supportive in the near term before gradually shifting towards recovery-driven investment themes following the stronger first quarter of 2026 (Q1) average daily value (ADV). The investment bank revised its financial year of 2026 (FY26) ADV assumption to RM3.15 billion or an increase of 11 per cent from RM2.85 billion. Kenanga IB said Bursa's Q1 ADV beat its estimates by 11 per cent, driven by heightened market volatility arising from geopolitical conflicts and inflation concerns.

items.title: Pakistan in spotlight as US, Iran set for direct talks to end conflict
items.summary: KARACHI: All eyes are on Pakistan as the United States and Iran are set to hold rare direct talks in Islamabad starting Saturday amid a fragile truce between the two sides, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported. Ahmad said the prospects of Saturday talks look "more promising" now after Israel agreed Thursday to hold direct talks with Lebanon, a day after Israeli forces carried out deadly strikes across the Middle East country. The meetings between the US and the Iranian delegations will include direct talks in Islamabad towards achieving a "permanent" ceasefire, Pakistani government sources earlier told Anadolu.

items.title: Eleven-state e-waste crackdown nets RM582m haul, 515 arrests in major bust
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Authorities have carried out a major nationwide crackdown on illegal e-waste activities under Op Hazard 3.0, with hundreds arrested and large-scale seizures recorded across multiple states. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said the operation involved coordinated enforcement across 11 states and 110 targeted locations. Mohd Khalid said 2,147 police officers and personnel from multiple enforcement agencies were deployed throughout the operation.

items.title: Anwar: Death of Iran's Kamal Kharazi is loss of 'exceptional diplomat'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has paid tribute to former Iranian foreign minister Kamal Kharazi and extended condolences to his family and the people of Iran. "I was deeply saddened to learn of the passing of Dr Kamal Kharazi, former Foreign Minister of Iran whose untimely death came from the wounds inflicted by Zionist terrorist attacks," he said in a social media post. Kharazi, died Thursday from wounds suffered in US-Israeli strikes on Apr 1, Iranian media reported.

items.title: Chery Malaysia unveils Carlinko mobile app for PHEVs
items.summary: CHERY Malaysia has introduced a new mobile application, CarLinko, for owners of its plug-in hybrid electric vehicle (PHEV) models as it looks to enhance digital integration and user convenience. The app is currently available for owners of the Tiggo 7 PHEV and Tiggo 8 PHEV, which were launched last year. CarLinko allows users to remotely access and monitor various vehicle functions.

items.title: Rafizi receives second PKR show-cause letter, denies allegations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pandan member of parliament Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli has submitted a formal reply to a second show-cause letter issued by PKR disciplinary board dated April 9, defending his recent remarks and denying allegations against him. Rafizi said his criticism of what he described as a "culture of sycophancy" within the party was not new and had been consistently raised directly with party leadership during previous congresses. I have consistently raised it directly with the party president," he said.

items.title: China's yuan steady but set for biggest weekly rise in 15 months
items.summary: SHANGHAI: China's yuan held steady against the dollar on Friday but looked set for the biggest weekly rise in nearly 15 months, as a two-week ceasefire deal between the US and Iran and a pick-up in domestic inflation lifted market sentiment. "China needs reflation, and rising oil prices are actually accelerating this process," said OCBC Asia macro research head Tommy Xie. "However, this oil-price-driven reflation is not of high quality, what we hope for is demand-driven reflation ... Once inflation picks up, reshaping inflation expectations would not be a bad thing for the market."

items.title: Malaysian Bar moves to challenge AG’s NFA decision in Zahid corruption case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — The Malaysian Bar is seeking a judicial review to quash the attorney-general’s (AG) decision not to continue criminal proceedings against Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. The Bar is also seeking a mandamus order compelling the AG to resume prosecution or reinstate charges against Zahid under Section 254A of the CPC. According to the news report, the Bar asserted that the discontinuance contradicts the High Court’s earlier finding that the prosecution had established a prima facie case against Zahid.

items.title: Fahmi offers condolences over RTM interpreter Abby Tan's death
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has extended his condolences to the family of Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) sign language interpreter Mohammad Fadhil Tan Razman Tan, who died today. In a post on Threads, Fahmi conveyed his sympathies to the late interpreter's family, noting that Mohammad Fadhil, who served with RTM, had died today. "Condolences also to colleagues in RTM, especially in the Current Affairs News Division," he said.

items.title: RTM sign language interpreter Abby Tan dies a month after road accident, minister extends condolences
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has extended his condolences to the family of Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) sign language interpreter Mohammad Fadhil Tan Razman Tan, who died today. He said Mohammad Fadhil, better known as Abby Tan, had been involved in a road accident on March 6. Fahmi also expressed his condolences to Mohammad Fadhil’s colleagues at RTM, especially in the News and Current Affairs Department, and called on the public to pray that his soul be blessed and placed among the righteous.

items.title: Coffee and biscuit products withdrawn over halal issue in Melaka
items.summary: JAIM's Halal Management Division said inspections at 33 supermarkets across the state found the products were no longer on shelves. Last Wednesday, a coffee and biscuit processing factory here lost its halal certification after it was found using brushes made of pig bristles to clean equipment. Enquiries can be made to JAIM's Halal Management Division at 06-333 3333 (extensions 4055, 4056, or 4060).

items.title: Over 5,000 litres of subsidised diesel seized in Perak raid over alleged profiteering scheme
items.summary: IPOH, April 10 — The Perak Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) seized 5,070 litres of subsidised diesel in a raid at Kampung Raja Hitam near Ayer Tawar early today. He said the raided timber and hardware premises were believed to have been used as a transit point to illegally accumulate diesel supplies before redistributing them to the industrial market. “A man was found transferring liquid believed to be diesel from a three-tonne lorry into an intermediate bulk container (IBC).

items.title: 'Time of Use' scheme cuts bill: 128,868 consumers have switched - Energy ministry
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The government is encouraging Malaysians to switch to the Time of Use (ToU) scheme to cut electricity bills and boost national energy efficiency amid volatile global energy prices. In a statement today, the Energy Transition and Water Transformation Ministry said 128,868 consumers had already switched to the ToU scheme as of February, a clear sign of growing public awareness of smarter energy management. The ministry said the scheme offers lower rates during off-peak hours and higher rates during peak hours.

items.title: 35 to be charged over Cheras entertainment outlet attack [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Thirty-five individuals linked to a violent attack on an entertainment outlet in Cheras arrived at the Kuala Lumpur courts complex here today to be charged. The suspects, comprising men of various ages, were brought in under heavy police escort at 11.25am. They were seen handcuffed as police officers armed with sub-machine guns escorted them into the court building amid tight security.

items.title: No proof MACC involved in ‘corporate mafia’ case, says IGP as probe continues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail today said there is no evidence connecting the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) to any “corporate mafia” activity as alleged. But he said investigations are ongoing and several people have been identified for questioning, both in Malaysia and overseas. He said cases involving foreign jurisdictions will require government-to-government cooperation as well as agency-to-agency.

items.title: Kuala Pilah man attacks brother with machete after dispute
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 10 — A man was injured after being attacked with a machete by his older brother in a scuffle at a house in Taman Air Mawang, Johol, here yesterday. Kuala Pilah district police chief Superintendent Muhamad Mustafah Hussin said police received a report on the incident involving the 27-year-old victim at 3.49pm. “The victim’s brother later came to the house with a machete and attacked him.

items.title: Iran's supreme leader vows 'new phase' in Hormuz management
items.summary: ISTANBUL: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Hosseini Khamenei said in a message Thursday that the management of the Strait of Hormuz will "certainly enter a new phase," while vowing that Teheran will demand compensation for all war-related damages, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported. Khamenei said Iran will move toward a new strategic phase with the Strait of Hormuz. Khamenei stressed that Iran will pursue legal and material accountability for damages.

items.title: Sarawak trials edible rabies vaccines to boost immunisation for free-roaming dogs
items.summary: KUCHING, April 10 — Sarawak has begun distributing oral rabies vaccine (ORV) bait to free-roaming dogs as part of efforts to strengthen rabies control measures in the state. During the ORV briefing and training programme held near the Padawan Municipal Council (MPP) here, Department of Veterinary Services Sarawak (DVSS) deputy director Dr Nicholas Jenek said the programme involves giving edible vaccine bait to dogs that are difficult to catch or handle. Dr Ad Vos delivers a briefing on the oral rabies vaccine (ORV) programme during the session.

items.title: Japan's Nikkei jumps as oil stabilises before Iran talks
items.summary: TOKYO: Japan's Nikkei share average surged on Friday and was on track for its sharpest weekly gain in eight months, as optimism over corporate earnings and technology investment outweighed concerns about a fragile ceasefire in the Middle East. The benchmark Nikkei 225 Index rose 1.65 per cent to 56,817.54, poised for a 6.8 per cent weekly jump that would be its best since mid-August. The broader Topix edged up just 0.05 per cent to 3,743.17.

items.title: Palm muted ahead of MPOB, cargo surveyor data
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian palm oil futures traded in a tight range on Friday ahead of Malaysian Palm Oil Board and cargo surveyor data, but were set for a weekly fall after five straight weeks of gains. The benchmark palm oil contract for June delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange slid RM7, or 0.15 per cent, to RM4,636 (US$1,168.05) a tonne in early trade. Dalian's most-active soyoil contract rose 0.55 per cent, while its palm oil contract added 1.01 per cent.

items.title: Stocks shaky as Israeli attacks on Lebanon tests Iran ceasefire
items.summary: Investors were nervous as Iran cited Israel's ongoing attacks on Lebanon as a key sticking point in its agreement with the US MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan gained 0.5 per cent, led by a 1.9 per cent jump for South Korea's Kospi. Japan's Nikkei 225 tacked on 1.5 per cent, while S&P 500 e-mini futures reversed earlier losses to trade flat. Bitcoin was down 0.7 per cent at US$71,903.27, while ether was 1.0 per cent lower at US$2,191.81.

items.title: SpaceX posted nearly US$5 billion loss in 2025, The Information reports
items.summary: NEW YORK: Elon Musk's IPO-bound SpaceX posted a loss of nearly US$5 billion in 2025 on revenue of more than US$18.5 billion, The Information reported on Thursday, citing sources. Reuters could not immediately verify the report. The company, which confidentially filed for a US listing in March, generated about US$8 billion in profit last year on revenue of US$15 billion to US$16 billion, Reuters reported in January.

items.title: Al Rajhi unit buys i-City commercial asset
items.summary: The asset is located opposite the group's AI Living component and adjacent to Central i-City Mall, placing it within a cluster that combines commercial, residential and digital infrastructure. I-Bhd said participation by financial institutions is contributing to a growing concentration of banking and financial services within the development. "The participation of global financial institutions adds an important institutional dimension to our ecosystem, complementing the broader mix of residential, commercial and digital infrastructure within the development."

items.title: Malaysia PET plastic prices up 40pc as shortage hits packaging supply chain
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Malaysia is beginning to feel the impact of a tightening plastic packaging market, with PET resin shortages and rising prices of up to 40 per cent already disrupting supply and hitting retail availability. Malaysia Plastic Manufacturers Association (Johor chapter) chairman Mike Tan said PET production is directly dependent on crude oil supply, meaning any disruption in crude flows quickly translates into reduced packaging output. He said manufacturers are already raising plastic prices by between 15 per cent and 40 per cent, depending on material type, as supply tightens.

items.title: Kidurong woman dies after car skids, crashes into guardrail
items.summary: SELANGAU, April 10 — A 34-year-old woman was killed in a single-vehicle accident on the Pan Borneo Highway at KM65 Sibu-Bintulu road near Sungai Kua early today. Mukah police chief DSP Muhamad Rizal Alias said the incident occurred at around 1.40am when the victim was driving by herself in a hatchback car from Bintulu to Sibu. “The victim, a local woman from RPR Kidurong, Bintulu, sustained severe injuries to the head, body, and right leg, and was pronounced dead at the scene,” he said.

items.title: Trailer driver survives Jasin highway crash, only to die while crossing road
items.summary: JASIN, April 10 — A 58-year-old trailer driver died from being run over by a lorry while attempting to cross the North-South Expressway (southbound) following an earlier crash yesterday afternoon, the New Straits Times reported today. After the crash, the man exited his vehicle and tried to cross the highway from the right lane towards the left side of the road. The lorry was unable to avoid the trailer driver or brake in time and hit the man crossing the road, the senior police officer said.

items.title: Sabah rep files private member's motion demanding 40pc federal revenue share be enforced immediately
items.summary: PENAMPANG, April 10 — Kapayan assemblyman Chin Teck Ming has filed a Private Member’s Motion in the Sabah Legislative Assembly calling for the immediate implementation of the state’s constitutional right to 40 per cent of federal revenue derived from Sabah. Chin said the motion, submitted to the State Assembly Speaker on April 2, seeks for Sabah to receive its entitlement under Article 112C and the Tenth Schedule of the Federal Constitution. However, Chin estimated that federal revenue collected from Sabah — including income tax, customs duties and petroleum-related income — ranges between RM20 billion and RM30 billion annually.

items.title: Sabah oil and gas sales tax revenue expected to fall, says deputy CM
items.summary: KUNDASANG, April 10 — Sabah is expected to collect less from its State Sales Tax (SST) on the oil and gas sector this year due to declining production and fluctuating global prices. Deputy Chief Minister II Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun said collections, which topped RM2 billion last year, are unlikely to reach the same level in 2026. Masidi highlighted the contributions of industry players such as Hibiscus Petroleum Bhd and Petronas in strengthening Sabah’s economic and social programs.

items.title: Ampang-bound LRT trains turn back at Chan Sow Lin, commuters told to transfer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — The Ampang/Sri Petaling LRT line is operating on modified services after a track switch failure at Chan Sow Lin Station, with commuters required to change trains or adjust their journeys, Rapid Rail said today. The rail operator said trains from Ampang are turning back at Chan Sow Lin at about six-minute intervals, while services from Sentul Timur to Putra Heights continue to operate as normal. Passengers travelling towards Ampang are required to transfer at Chan Sow Lin Station using Platforms 1B and 2B, while those heading from Ampang to Sentul Timur must change at Platform 1A.

items.title: Police nab nine in Teluk Intan drug bust with cannabis, meth and heroin
items.summary: IPOH, April 10 — A woman was among nine people, believed to be drug addicts, arrested in an operation to curb drug abuse around the Labu Kubong area, near Teluk Intan, Wednesday. Hilir Perak police chief ACP Chua Kok Lian said all nine suspects were Malaysians aged between 29 and 37. He said that a team from the Narcotics Crime Investigation Division of the Hilir Perak Police Headquarters, which carried out the operation, also seized various types of drugs, including 20.48 grammes of ganja, 21.15 grammes of syabu, 2.64 grammes of heroin and 0.34 grammes of Erimin 5 pills.

items.title: Six-year-old boy drowns after falling into canal in Sungai Dua
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS, April 10 — A six-year-old boy drowned after falling into the Sungai Dua canal at Jalan Bertam Perdana 2 here yesterday. Penang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant director (Operations) John Sagun Francis said the victim, Muhammad Aryan Hakim Muhammad Jamaluddin, was found floating about three kilometres from where he was reported to have fallen. He said the department received a call at 5.03pm and dispatched a team from the Bertam and Kepala Batas fire and rescue stations to the scene.

items.title: Cost squeeze hits vegetable farmers, some scaling back or exit industry
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 10 — Vegetable farmers are cutting back production as rising fuel and fertiliser costs, coupled with prolonged dry weather, squeeze margins across the sector, The Star reported today. Federation of Vegetable Farmers Associations president Lim Ser Kwee said around 20 per cent of more than 6,000 members nationwide have already reduced output, with some considering shifting to other crops such as oil palm. “The increase is too much for farmers to absorb, especially since many vegetable farmers are not eligible for diesel subsidies.

items.title: Police hunt five men over back-to-back armed robberies in Nilai
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 10 — Police are tracking down five men to assist in investigations into two separate armed robbery incidents in Bandar Baru Nilai and the Rumah Rakyat Nilai area here last night. Nilai district police chief Supt Johari Yahya said the first incident was reported at 1.24am at a 24-hour convenience store on Jalan BBN 1/2F in Bandar Baru Nilai, adding that initial investigations found that the robbery occurred around 1.10am involving two unidentified men on a motorcycle. Meanwhile, police also received a report at 9.48pm of another armed robbery in the Rumah Rakyat Nilai housing area, involving three men on two motorcycles carrying machetes.

items.title: Two-acre forest fire in Seri Kembangan brought under control, says Fire Dept
items.summary: Selangor Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations Division assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the department received an emergency call regarding the incident at 9.27pm. According to Ahmad Mukhlis, the fire involved a hilly forest area estimated at two acres. He said four fire engines were deployed in the operation — three Fire Rescue Tender (FRT) units and one Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) unit.

items.title: Pakistani sexual offence suspect escapes Sungai Buloh Prison, manhunt on
items.summary: SUNGAI BULOH, April 10 — Police are tracking a Pakistani remand detainee who escaped from Sungai Buloh Prison yesterday. Sungai Buloh district police chief Superintendent Mohd Hafiz Muhammad Nor said the suspect, identified as Muhammad Hassan, 24, is wanted in connection with several serious offences, including sexual crimes involving a child. Given the nature of the offences, police advise the public to exercise caution while the suspect remains at large.

items.title: PM Anwar calls for lower recruitment fees for Bangladeshi workers in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Malaysia has asked the government of Bangladesh to consider measures to reduce the cost of hiring Bangladeshi workers. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, in a Facebook post yesterday, said the move is aimed at the welfare of workers. During the meeting, Anwar also repeated his congratulations to Tarique Rahman, who was appointed Prime Minister of Bangladesh on February 17.

items.title: Ulu Tiram attacker’s father claimed police blood ‘halal’ to be shed, court hears
items.summary: Muhamad Farhan Haiqim Mohd Isa, 24, said the first accused, Radin Imran Radin Mohd Yassin, 64, personally told him that while they were detained together at the Seri Alam Police lock-up in Masai, Johor, following the May 2024 attack. In the 2.30am incident on May 17, 2024, two police constables, Ahmad Azza Fahmi Azhar, 22, and Muhamad Syafiq Ahmad Said, 24, were killed, while Corporal Mohd Hasif Roslan, 40, was injured after being shot. The attacker, 21-year-old Radin Luqman, was later shot dead.

items.title: KL police: Suspended Bukit Aman officer under probe in Kepong RM4.4m robbery spree
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Police have confirmed that a suspended senior officer arrested over a string of armed robberies in Kepong was previously attached to the federal police headquarters in Bukit Aman. City police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the suspect, a Deputy Superintendent of Police (DSP), had been serving in the Commercial Crime Investigation Department prior to his suspension on January 23, the New Straits Times reported today. The DSP was among six suspects — including two bodyguards — detained over multiple break-ins targeting expatriate homes in Kepong, Fadil previously said.

items.title: WFH for civil servants from next week won’t hurt economy, says Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — The implementation of the work-from-home (WFH) policy for civil servants beginning next Wednesday will not disrupt the nation’s economic activity, but instead aims to ensure productivity is maintained, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. Commenting on public concerns over the possibility of panic buying, Akmal Nasrullah said the government had taken early measures by reviewing supply issues since the activation of the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) early last month. He said among the aspects given attention was the assurance of food supply as well as essential goods.

items.title: ‘End the lies’: Melania Trump denies Epstein links, calls for hearings (VIDEO)
items.summary: WASHINGTON, April 10 — First lady Melania Trump denied on Thursday that she had any relationship with Jeffrey Epstein and said she was not one of his victims, thrusting the Epstein matter back into the spotlight after her husband had sought to put it behind him. “The lies linking me with the disgraceful Jeffrey Epstein need to end today,” Melania Trump said, reading a statement and declining to take questions from reporters. “I have never been friends with Epstein,” Melania Trump said.

items.title: Dzulkefly says settlement includes Facebook clarification, removal of disputed post by Najib
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad has withdrawn his civil defamation suit against former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak over allegations of nepotism involving the appointment of his daughter in a government-linked trust body. In a Facebook post yesterday, Dzulkefly said the settlement was reached before High Court Judge Mahazan Mat Taib after Najib agreed to immediately upload on his Facebook page a clarification statement read in court, as well as remove the related earlier post. He said Najib, in his court clarification, acknowledged his complaint that the posting had allegedly created the impression that he practised nepotism in relation to the appointment of his daughter, Nurul Iman.

items.title: When money feels tight, how will Malaysians vote for brands with their wallet?
items.summary: Research firm Rakuten Insight’s latest survey this month shows that Malaysians would not necessarily go for what is cheapest, and that they also want a good quality product when choosing a brand. “53.4 per cent cite lowest price as a driver. Buying the wrong cheap thing is an expensive mistake,” he added ‘Buatan Malaysia’ something Malaysians want in a financial squeeze In Rakuten Insight Malaysia’s Malaysia Cost Pressure Pulse (MCPP) April survey, 1,052 Malaysian adults were asked to choose what they consider when deciding on a brand, with the top five factors being: lowest price (53 per cent); promotions/ cashback (39 per cent); durable, long-term value (27 per cent); trusted quality (won’t risk wasting money) (26 per cent); and preference for local-made (18 per cent).

items.title: Malaysia orders halts to Malbatt patrols, seeks UN review as Lebanon mission grows unsafe
items.summary: REMBAU, April 9 — Malaysia will request the United Nations (UN) to review the terms of deployment for peacekeeping missions, including the Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) 850-13 under the UN Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL), following the worsening security situation in the country. Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said this was due to the current situation in Lebanon no longer being conducive for a peacekeeping mission, raising concerns over the safety of more than 500 Malbatt personnel stationed there. “The situation in Lebanon is no longer aligned with a peacekeeping mission.
